# GroundWork Documentation Index

The canonical documents in this project, in their expected order of
relevance for AI agents working in this codebase.

*The Vision-and-direction and System documents are created by the GroundWork
setup phases and appear here as each phase commits — those links resolve once
setup runs. The other sections ship at install time.*

## Vision and direction
- [Product Brief](docs/product-brief.md): The product's vision, users, and constraints.
- [Design System](docs/design-system.md): Interface decisions, NFRs, and design language.
- [Maturity Roadmap](docs/maturity.md): The project's assessment against the GroundWork maturity model and the tracked roadmap of open gaps.

## How we work
- [How we work](docs/ways-of-working/how-we-work.md): The two-mode lifecycle, the three delivery lanes (patch, quick bet, bet), and what proving a milestone at the real front door actually requires.
- [Units of work](docs/ways-of-working/units-of-work.md): What proves a milestone — the front-door definition, no feature flag — and what makes a slice vertical.
- [Documentation protocol](docs/ways-of-working/documentation.md): How living-document updates work, what groundwork-check flags versus leaves alone, and ADR numbering mechanics (the amend-vs-supersede doctrine lives at Architecture Decisions).

## Engineering principles
- [Manifesto](docs/principles/index.md): The nine beliefs every other page hangs off — start here; every other page is self-contained once you have this frame.
- [Code craft](docs/principles/foundations/code-craft.md): Simplicity is the default; abstraction is a cost that must be earned — the discipline of deletion and no speculative flexibility.
- [Documentation](docs/principles/foundations/documentation.md): Docs hold the knowledge, skills control the behaviour — Diátaxis structure, machine-readable exports, and freshness enforced wherever a human would drift.
- [Testing](docs/principles/foundations/testing.md): Risk-weighted assurance — honeycomb service tests, real dependencies, an independent oracle, and the front-door proof — not coverage targets.
- [Product engineering](docs/principles/foundations/product-engineering.md): Outcomes over outputs — shaped work, a fixed appetite over an estimate, and the refusal to let "shipped" replace "worked."
- [Continuous discovery](docs/principles/foundations/continuous-discovery.md): Discovery as a continuous habit, not a one-time phase — the opportunity-solution tree and staying in contact with the people you build for.
- [Product risks](docs/principles/foundations/product-risks.md): The four ways a bet fails — value, usability, feasibility, viability — and killing the riskiest assumption first, before delivery starts.
- [Success metrics](docs/principles/foundations/success-metrics.md): Design the measure before you ship — North Star and counter-metric paired, so a "no" answer is as informative as a "yes."
- [Requirements & specs](docs/principles/foundations/requirements-and-specs.md): Evidence-grounded, testable specs — jobs-to-be-done, stable IDs, and why a vague acceptance criterion gets silently filled by the model's own assumptions.
- [Prioritization & appetite](docs/principles/foundations/prioritization-and-appetite.md): Sizing work on worth and stakes, not effort — why AI broke effort as a reliable planning axis and what replaced it.
- [Reliability](docs/principles/quality/reliability.md): Error budgets, graceful degradation, and deliberate failure injection — reliability as a property designed in, not patched on after an incident.
- [Observability](docs/principles/quality/observability.md): OpenTelemetry-first instrumentation and traces as first-class test assertions — what to fix when you can't see why a system is misbehaving.
- [Performance](docs/principles/quality/performance.md): Latency as a budget spent deliberately across every hop, enforced in CI — tail latency and backpressure over "fast enough."
- [Security](docs/principles/quality/security.md): Zero-trust defaults — untrusted services, hostile inputs, compromised secrets — and a system that stays standing when any one control fails.
- [Privacy](docs/principles/quality/privacy.md): Privacy as a design input, decided before data is collected — some mistakes here (backups, warehouses, model training) can never be fully undone.
- [Accessibility](docs/principles/quality/accessibility.md): WCAG 2.2 AA as a shipped baseline, not after-launch polish — and, since the EAA, a legal floor in major markets.
- [The Day-2 Operational Baseline](docs/principles/delivery/day-2-operational-baseline.md): The stack-agnostic bar every project clears to be operable and debuggable on day two — what a forged (non-generator) stack must still earn.
- [Developer experience](docs/principles/delivery/devex.md): The inner loop as the biggest lever on team output — the dev CLI as the golden path, DORA/DevEx as how you tell if the loop is healthy.
- [Progressive delivery](docs/principles/delivery/progressive-delivery.md): Decoupling deploy from release — flags, canaries, and trunk-based development so shipping often doesn't mean shipping riskily.
- [Platform](docs/principles/delivery/platform.md): The platform as a product with users, a backlog, and a quality bar — when paving a path helps and when self-service just relocates the pain.
- [Cost engineering](docs/principles/delivery/cost-engineering.md): Cost as a non-functional requirement with a budget and a dashboard — made visible at the decision, not discovered in a quarterly bill.
- [How we structure code](docs/principles/system-design/code-structure.md): A pure core, swappable edges, no leaked implementation details — the single highest-leverage structural choice, non-negotiable for new code.
- [API design](docs/principles/system-design/api-design.md): Contract-first design — the API as the most durable commitment a service makes, shaped for the next ten versions and for agent consumers.
- [Integration patterns](docs/principles/system-design/integration-patterns.md): Choosing sync vs async, the outbox pattern, and idempotency by the guarantee an integration actually needs — not by what felt easy.
- [Real-time patterns](docs/principles/system-design/real-time.md): Real-time channels as long-lived contracts, not request/response — designed against the assumption the connection drops mid-flight.
- [Data engineering](docs/principles/system-design/data-engineering.md): Data outlives the service that wrote it — event and table contracts shaped for consumers that don't exist yet.
- [Architecture decisions](docs/principles/system-design/architecture-decisions.md): ADRs as governed, re-evaluable commitments — when to amend a record in place versus supersede it, and why the record is immutable but the decision is not.
- [Evolutionary architecture](docs/principles/system-design/evolutionary-architecture.md): Fitness functions that make an architectural rule executable — because a rule that lives only in a wiki is already being broken somewhere.
- [Surface architecture](docs/principles/system-design/surface-architecture.md): The BFF seam and why a surface is an adapter, never a place for business logic — read before adding a second surface.
- [Identity & access](docs/principles/system-design/identity-and-access.md): Identity decided at the architecture stage — OIDC/OAuth2.1 for humans, SPIFFE workload identity for services, agents as first-class non-human identities.
- [Durable execution](docs/principles/system-design/durable-execution.md): Workflow-as-code for a process that is multi-step, long-running, or must complete-or-compensate — when it earns its determinism contract over an outbox.
- [AI engineering](docs/principles/ai-native/ai-engineering.md): Prompts as code, evals as tests, context as a design surface — making a probabilistic system behave predictably enough to ship.
- [Agent-native systems](docs/principles/ai-native/agent-native-systems.md): Designing interfaces an agent can consume without a human translator — MCP, machine-readable docs, and an agent as an untrusted, retrying, context-limited caller.
- [Agentic systems](docs/principles/ai-native/agentic-systems.md): An agent loop is a distributed system with a non-deterministic core — topology, context as the scarce resource, durability, and guardrails sized to the stakes.
- [AI-native product](docs/principles/ai-native/ai-native-product.md): Product management for a probabilistic system — the outcome envelope, evals as a product responsibility, and the three AI cost layers standard prioritization misses.
- [Design foundations](docs/principles/design/design-foundations.md): Taste as an input, an implemented specification as the output — a design that only lives in a comp hasn't done its job.
- [Visual design](docs/principles/design/visual-design.md): Perceptual colour, typographic rigour, and modelled light — the craft details where an implemented design and a framework default actually diverge.
- [Layout & space](docs/principles/design/layout-and-space.md): A tokenised spatial scale and container-driven responsiveness — why device-width breakpoints can't make a component reusable.
- [Interaction & motion](docs/principles/design/interaction-and-motion.md): Every state a surface can be in — empty, loading, error, success — designed, not left to fall out of the code; motion that serves a function or is cut.
- [Usability & UX](docs/principles/design/usability-and-ux.md): Heuristics and UX laws as decision-time tools — applied while a screen is being designed, not audited after a usability test finds the problem.
- [Design systems & tokens](docs/principles/design/design-systems-and-tokens.md): The design system as a contract — tokens as wire format, components as typed interfaces — not a component library reproduced by hand.
- [AI-native design](docs/principles/design/ai-native-design.md): Designing for a distribution of outputs, not a single answer — latency, confidence, and failure as first-class design materials.
- [Postgres](docs/principles/stack/postgres.md): The default datastore absent a specific, written reason otherwise — schema, migrations, and pgvector as a production vector store.

## System
- [Architecture](docs/architecture/index.md): Service topology and communication patterns.
- [Infrastructure](docs/architecture/infrastructure.md): Boot order, ports, health checks, and run commands.
- [Domain](docs/architecture/domain/): Entity definitions, lifecycle state machines, and invariants.
- [Decisions](docs/architecture/decisions/): Architectural decision records.

## Stack principles (added by generators)
<!-- Stack-specific docs are appended here when a generator deploys them. -->
